name: Configure Motor Imagery Channel Protocol from EEG Recording
description: You are a BCI research scientist analyzing EEG data for a neural prosthetics study investigating motor imagery signals. Using the 8-channel EEG recording at ~/Documents/OpenBCI_GUI/Recordings/OpenBCI-EEG-S001-MotorImagery.txt (channels Fp1, Fp2, C3, C4, P7, P8, O1, O2), configure the OpenBCI GUI for motor cortex analysis. The configuration must isolate the C3 and C4 electrode channels, apply an 8–30 Hz bandpass filter, and include an FFT Plot for monitoring the frequency spectrum. Save the session configuration to ~/Documents/OpenBCI_GUI/Settings/motor_imagery_config.json. This protocol allows analysis of motor cortex ERD/ERS patterns while excluding contamination from frontal, parietal, and occipital channels.

metadata.recording_source: PhysioNet EEG Motor Movement/Imagery Dataset (eegmmidb 1.0.0), Subject S001
